screenshot_note: Puppeteer succeeded this cycle (--single-process flag resolved thread limits). Fresh desktop (1280x800) and mobile (375x812) screenshots captured from the live site. Bloberto speech bubble escape confirmed visually at top of office floor. Footer counter '1/9' confirmed visible. Everything else assessed from source code.
verified_fixes: Nothing. Zero. The entire QA51 suggestion list carries forward unchanged. The three pre-QA51 fixes (onResume scoping, paused-pill clickability guard, empty task guard) remain intact from before. Everything Carlos was asked to do in QA51 is still sitting in the backlog, untouched. The inline toast styles have now been in the 'to fix' column for six consecutive reviews. The app is stable — nothing broke. Nothing improved either.
carry_overs: STRIKE 6 — Duplicate inline toast styles: App.jsx lines 1120-1143 (completionToast) and Office.jsx lines 1217-1240 (plantToast) still have 12+ near-identical inline style objects each. ShortcutToast — in the same file — uses a CSS class correctly. The developer demonstrably knows the right pattern. These two toasts are now a performance art piece about selective attention.

STRIKE 5 — Activity filter role=radiogroup buttons have no arrow-key navigation. App.jsx lines 1324-1342 and 1343-1363. Still just tab/click. The ARIA contract for radiogroup requires arrow-key cycling. Still not delivered. Assistive technology users encounter a broken interaction every time they reach this section.

STRIKE 5 — Mobile office floor text is physically unreadable at 375px. Workers, desks, nameplates, whiteboard content all compress to sub-pixel sizes. Confirmed in fresh 375x812 screenshot. The floor looks busy in all the wrong ways on mobile.

STRIKE 5 — Bloberto speech bubble escapes the office boundary at top. Confirmed in fresh desktop AND mobile screenshots — the bubble 'Wait you shipped at THIS hour?! Legends. 🏆' is visibly floating above the office floor border. Bloberto is positioned at top: ~4%, bubble renders above. Five reviews. The bubble still escapes.

STRIKE 4 — Footer tagline pagination counter still visible. Fresh screenshot confirms '1/9' at opacity 0.55 next to the rotating quote. The taglines are great. The progress indicator turns them into a content carousel. Still paginated.

STRIKE 3 — Triple visibilitychange listeners still active. App.jsx line 850 (handleVisibilityResume — calls onResume() unconditionally on EVERY tab-show, even after a 2-second tab switch), line 873 (restarts poll interval), line 1074 (updates document.title). Handler at line 850 calls onResume() without checking whether polling was actually paused. Every tab switch triggers an eager sync that bypasses the idle-timeout guard.

STRIKE 3 — sync-status--paused div (App.jsx line 482) and polling-paused-pill span (line 1170) both still lack role='button', tabIndex, and onKeyDown. Keyboard users cannot resume polling. The paused-pill also has onClick inline with aria-live which is semantically incoherent — aria-live is for announcing dynamic changes, not for labeling interactive elements.

STRIKE 3 — Office.css duplicate vibe selectors. Lines 91-92 use single-quote attribute selectors for .office-floor[data-vibe='crushing'] and [data-vibe='on-fire']; lines 97-98 use double-quote selectors for the same elements and additionally set --grid-line. Lines 91-92 are dead code.

STRIKE 3 — ActivityLog spreads and reverses the full entries array before slicing. App.jsx line 135: [...entries].reverse().slice(0, 20). Should be entries.slice(-20).reverse(). This copies up to 50 entries on every render of the log.

STRIKE 3 — getRelativeTime still duplicated inline in Office.jsx ghost bubble IIFE (lines 702-710). The identical 7-line logic exists in App.jsx at line 55. utils/ exists and is imported. The function belongs in utils/time.js. Third time asking.

STRIKE 3 — isDimmed still incorrectly dims workers with no current task. getTaskTags(null) returns [] → .some() returns false → isDimmed = true whenever a tag filter is active. An idle worker with no task gets greyed out as if they don't match the filter. Fix: add !!w.task guard.

STRIKE 2 — Worker card flip has no focus management. When the ℹ️ button flips the card, focus stays on the button which is now inside the aria-hidden front panel. The back panel's Close button never receives focus. Keyboard users flip the card and are immediately stranded. WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order violation.

STRIKE 2 — Footer tagline span (App.jsx line 1384-1390) has cursor:pointer, onClick, and title='tap for another thought' but no tabIndex, role, or onKeyDown. Keyboard users can neither discover nor activate quote cycling.
STRIKE 2 — ErrorBoundary (App.jsx lines 7-19) has getDerivedStateFromError but no componentDidCatch. Production errors are silently swallowed. No console log, no stack trace, no error reporting hook.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 2: Worker card flip strands keyboard focus in aria-hidden panel
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 305: setIsFlipped(true) flips the card but focus stays on the ℹ️ button, which is now inside the aria-hidden front panel (line 295: aria-hidden={isFlipped || undefined}). A keyboard user has just activated a button that made the focused element invisible to the accessibility tree. Fix: add a closeButtonRef and a useEffect that calls closeButtonRef.current?.focus() when isFlipped transitions to true. On close (line 355), return focus to the ℹ️ button via a similar ref. This is WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order. It is also the minimum expected behavior from any flipcard pattern. The fix is 8 lines.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 2: Footer tagline span is a secret button that keyboard users can't find
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1384-1390: the footer-tagline span has style={{ cursor: 'pointer' }}, onClick, and title='tap for another thought' — but no tabIndex, no role, no onKeyDown. Keyboard users can neither discover the feature nor trigger it. Fix: convert to a <button>, or add tabIndex={0}, role='button', aria-label='Cycle to next tagline', and onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') setQuoteBonus(p => p + 1) }}. While you're there, either hide the ' {n}/{total}' counter (STRIKE 4) or change it to aria-label on the button so the count is accessible without being visually intrusive.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 6: Inline toast styles — the number speaks for itself
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1120-1143 (completionToast) and Office.jsx lines 1217-1240 (plantToast): 12+ inline style properties each, near-identical, for fixed-position notification toasts. ShortcutToast — added after both — uses .shortcut-toast CSS class correctly. Extract a .toast-popup class with the shared styles (position, background, border-radius, padding, font, box-shadow base, z-index, animation). Keep only dynamic border-color as inline. Also: both toasts share zIndex:9999 at fixed bottom-center — if a worker ships while the plant levels up they render on top of each other invisibly. The CSS class can also handle stacking with a transform offset. Six reviews. The suggestion text now has more history than most PRs.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 5: Activity filter radiogroup still missing arrow-key navigation
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1324-1342 and 1343-1363: two filter bars use role='radiogroup' with role='radio' on each button and aria-checked. The ARIA spec for radiogroup requires arrow keys to cycle between options — Tab enters the group, arrow keys navigate within it. Current implementation ignores arrow keys. Screen readers will announce these as radio buttons and users will expect navigation that never comes. Fix option A (recommended): change role to 'toolbar' with role='button' + aria-pressed, which accurately describes toggle buttons without the arrow-key contract. Fix option B: add onKeyDown to the radiogroup divs capturing ArrowLeft/ArrowRight to cycle selection — same pattern as handleTabKeyDown at line 752. Either is fine. Standing here doing nothing is not.

suggestions.title: NEW: stat-dispatch 'Scroll to workers' is another keyboard dead zone
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 469-473: the active stat card's dispatch span has style={{ cursor: 'pointer' }}, onClick, and title='Scroll to workers' — but no tabIndex, no role, no onKeyDown. It's the same pattern as polling-paused-pill and sync-status--paused, both of which are on the STRIKE 3 carry-over list. Fix: either wrap in a <button> or add tabIndex={0}, role='button', onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') document.querySelector('.workers-grid')?.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth' }) }}. This is the fourth interactive element in the app styled as a span with an onClick and no keyboard support. It's a pattern problem at this point.

suggestions.title: NEW: Activity log filter predicate written out twice
suggestions.description: The exact same filter logic for activityFilter + workerFilter is written in full at App.jsx lines 1309-1321 (for the event count display in the section-header) and again at lines 1364-1372 (for the entries passed to ActivityLog). If a new filter type is added, it must be added in two places. If one is changed and the other isn't, the count and the actual log will disagree. Fix: extract a const filteredActivity = useMemo(() => activityLog.filter(...), [activityLog, activityFilter, workerFilter]) above the JSX, then use filteredActivity.length for the count and filteredActivity for the log. One source of truth.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 2: ErrorBoundary has no componentDidCatch — errors vanish silently
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 7-19: the ErrorBoundary class has getDerivedStateFromError (correct) but no componentDidCatch(error, errorInfo). When the app crashes in production, the user sees 'Something went wrong — refresh the page' and you see nothing. No console log, no stack trace, no error detail. In dev, React's overlay covers this. In production you're flying blind. Fix: add componentDidCatch(error, errorInfo) { console.error('ErrorBoundary:', error, errorInfo) } at minimum. When an error reporting service is eventually added, this is the hook. The error message div also uses 5 inline styles — add a .error-boundary-message CSS class.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 5: Bloberto speech bubble escapes office boundary — screenshot proof, again
suggestions.description: Fresh desktop and mobile screenshots from this review cycle both show the speech bubble floating above the office floor's top border. Bloberto is at ~top:4%, the bubble renders above the character with no clipping. The office-floor has overflow:visible. Fix options: (a) flip bubble to render below the character when top < 15% (CSS position conditional via a data attribute); (b) move Bloberto down to top:10-12%, which still reads as 'top of office' visually; (c) add overflow:hidden to office-floor and accept that any above-character bubble gets clipped. Option (b) is one line. It's been five reviews.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 3: Triple visibilitychange listeners — one of them fires unconditionally
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 849-853: handleVisibilityResume calls onResume() on EVERY tab-show event regardless of whether the user was idle. onResume() calls setPollingPaused(false) + syncFromGitHub(). So: switch away from tab for 3 seconds, switch back — you get an immediate sync and the idle timer resets. This bypasses the 10-minute IDLE_TIMEOUT guard in pollTick entirely. Fix for line 850: add a pollingPaused check — if (!document.hidden && pollingPaused) onResume(). Or consolidate all three visibilitychange handlers (lines 850, 873, 1074) into one. Three separate useEffect instances registering to the same document event on a single component is code smell regardless.

suggestions.title: NEW: completionToast timer ref clobbers itself when multiple workers ship simultaneously
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 950-960: when justShipped.length > 1, each worker's shipping animation schedules via setTimeout(() => { setCompletionToast(...); completionToastTimerRef.current = setTimeout(() => setCompletionToast(null), 3000) }, i * 2600). Each worker's inner timer assignment overwrites completionToastTimerRef.current. Worker 0's cleanup timer (fires at t+3000ms) will set completionToast to null, cutting off worker 1's toast at t+2600ms before its own 3000ms cleanup fires. Worker 1 gets a truncated toast. In practice with only 5 workers this is a rare edge case but it's a logic bug. Fix: generate per-toast unique timer IDs or use a queue pattern instead of shared ref.

suggestions.title: NEW: Sparkline legend pills use array index as React key
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 416: {history.slice(-3).map((h, i) => (<span key={i} className='sparkline-legend__pill' ...>))} — using array index as key means React can't correctly reconcile if the slice changes. When history grows and the displayed window shifts, pill 0 (index 0) might represent a different vibe entry than before, causing stale DOM recycling. Fix: use key={h.ts} since each history entry has a timestamp and timestamps are unique. One character change per key prop.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 3: sync-status--paused and polling-paused-pill still keyboard-inaccessible
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 482: sync-status div has onClick={pollingPaused ? onResume : undefined} but no role, tabIndex, or onKeyDown. Line 1170: polling-paused-pill span has onClick and aria-live but no role, tabIndex, or onKeyDown. Note on the pill: aria-live announces dynamic content changes — it doesn't make an element interactive. The pill needs role='button' tabIndex={0} AND onKeyDown separately. Fix both: when pollingPaused is true, add role='button' tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') onResume() }}.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 3: Utility functions still not extracted to src/utils/
suggestions.description: App.jsx defines getRelativeTime() at line 55, formatDuration() at line 243, formatIdleDuration() at line 424. Office.jsx re-implements getRelativeTime as an inline IIFE at lines 702-710. utils/ has vibe.js, constants.js, and quotes.js — already extracted. The time utilities belong in utils/time.js. Would also enable unit testing of the time formatting logic and eliminate the Office.jsx IIFE duplication in one move.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 3: isDimmed incorrectly dims workers with no current task
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1263, 1286, 1298: isDimmed={selectedTag !== null && !getTaskTags(w.task).some(t => t.label === selectedTag)}. When w.task is null, getTaskTags returns [] and .some() returns false, so isDimmed becomes true. An unassigned worker gets greyed out whenever any tag filter is active. They're not 'not matching the filter' — they simply have no task. Fix: isDimmed={selectedTag !== null && !!w.task && !getTaskTags(w.task).some(t => t.label === selectedTag)}. Add the !!w.task guard. Three lines, three reviews.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 5: Mobile office floor text remains illegible at 375px
suggestions.description: Fresh 375px screenshot confirms: desk nameplates, COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT label, worker role text, whiteboard content — all sub-pixel or barely legible. The floor works fine as ambient decoration. It fails as informational content. Three viable options: (a) @media (max-width: 480px) rules to scale text to minimum 0.55rem across all office-floor text elements — fastest; (b) give office-floor a min-width:600px and allow overflow-x:auto scroll within its container; (c) add a mobile-simplified layout mode. Option (a) is a morning's work.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 4: Footer tagline pagination counter still there, still killing the magic
suggestions.description: Fresh screenshot confirms ' 1/9' in the footer at opacity 0.55. The taglines are genuinely good — 'the standup is 15 minutes. the anxiety is forever' deserves to land as a discovered moment, not a content carousel with a progress bar. The counter converts an easter egg into a UI widget. Remove it entirely, or show it only on hover with CSS transition. The ✦ sparkle on first visit is the right energy. The '1/9' is the wrong energy.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 3: Office.css duplicate vibe selectors — lines 91-92 are dead code
suggestions.description: Office.css lines 91-92 define .office-floor[data-vibe='crushing'] and [data-vibe='on-fire'] with single-quote attribute selectors. Lines 97-98 define the same selectors with double-quote attribute selectors (CSS treats both identically), and additionally set --grid-line. The double-quote rules come later in the cascade and win on all shared properties. Lines 91-92 set box-shadow and border-color; lines 97-98 set the same values plus --grid-line. Lines 91-92 are dead code. Delete them.

summary: Score drops from 3.7 to 3.5. Fresh screenshots captured this cycle — Puppeteer finally cooperated. The visual confirmation is not welcome news: Bloberto's speech bubble is still floating above the office floor border (STRIKE 5), the footer counter '1/9' is still plainly visible (STRIKE 4), and the mobile layout still has the same compressed-text issues (STRIKE 5). The entire QA51 list carried forward unchanged. Inline toast styles hit STRIKE 6. The radio group arrow-key gap and mobile floor text both hit STRIKE 5. Three items from QA51's new findings (worker card flip focus trap, footer span keyboard gap, ErrorBoundary silence) are now at STRIKE 2 having been completely ignored. Accessibility score drops from 2.5 to 2.0 — not because new issues were introduced, but because nothing was fixed and the cumulative picture is genuinely bad for keyboard users: there are now at least six interactive elements in the app with onClick but no keyboard handler. Code quality drops from 3.0 to 2.5 with the addition of a new DRY violation (duplicate activity filter predicate), sparkline index keys, and a multi-toast timer race condition. Four new findings this cycle. The app is still visually delightful and functionally solid — the features are good and the fun factor remains perfect. But the gap between the visual polish and the keyboard/screen reader experience keeps widening. Assign carlos a focused half-day on accessibility before QA53 or the a11y score hits 1.5.
